skill.name: calc
skill.description: "Spreadsheet creation, format conversion (ODS/XLSX/CSV), formulas, data automation with LibreOffice Calc."
skill.summary: "Spreadsheet creation, format conversion (ODS/XLSX/CSV), formulas, data automation with LibreOffice ...
skill.author: unknown

security_audit.summary: This skill has low-risk features: network. No dangerous patterns detected.

content.user_title: Master LibreOffice Calc automation and format conversion
content.value_statement: Automate spreadsheet creation, conversion, and data processing using LibreOffice Calc. Supports ODS, XLSX, CSV, and PDF formats via CLI or Python UNO, enabling efficient batch operations and reporting without manual intervention.
actual_capabilities: Create ODS spreadsheets from scratch or templates
Convert between ODS, XLSX, CSV, PDF, and HTML formats
Execute batch file conversions via command line
Automate data entry and formula calculations using Python UNO
Generate pivot tables and perform statistical analysis
Run LibreOffice Calc in headless mode for server-side tasks
limitations: Requires LibreOffice installation on the host system
Headless mode may require specific display settings (Xvfb) on Linux servers
Complex macro support (VBA) is limited compared to MS Excel
Python UNO scripting has a steeper learning curve than simple CSV libraries
Performance can be slower than dedicated Python libraries (like pandas) for huge datasets

use_cases.title: Automated Report Generation
use_cases.description: Generate monthly ODS reports from CSV data sources and convert them to PDF for distribution using scheduled scripts.

use_cases.title: Batch Format Conversion
use_cases.description: Convert legacy Excel files to the open ODS format or CSV for archiving and system integration without opening a GUI.

use_cases.title: Server-Side Spreadsheet Processing
use_cases.description: Use Python UNO to manipulate spreadsheet data, apply formulas, and export results dynamically in a web application backend.

prompt_templates.title: Convert File Format
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to change a spreadsheet file type.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert the file 'data.ods' to 'data.xlsx' using LibreOffice headless mode.

prompt_templates.title: Create ODS with Data
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to generate a new spreadsheet.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new ODS spreadsheet named 'report.ods', populate cell A1 with 'Sales', and A2 with '1000', then save it.

best_practices: Use named ranges instead of hardcoded cell references for better maintainability.
Run LibreOffice in headless mode (--headless) for server environments to avoid GUI dependencies.
Test format conversions thoroughly, as complex formatting may not transfer perfectly between ODS and XLSX.
Use CSV for intermediate data exchange when possible to ensure maximum compatibility.
Keep ODS source files in version control rather than binary XLSX files.
anti_patterns: Attempting to run GUI-dependent operations without a virtual display (Xvfb) on Linux servers.
Using LibreOffice Calc for simple CSV manipulation when standard text tools would be faster.
Hardcoding file paths in Python scripts instead of using environment variables or arguments.
Forgetting to close the document object in Python UNO scripts, leading to memory leaks.

faq.question: How do I run this on a server without a monitor?
faq.answer: Use the --headless flag and potentially Xvfb on Linux to simulate a display environment.

faq.question: Can it edit existing Excel files?
faq.answer: Yes, you can convert XLSX to ODS, edit it, and convert back, though some advanced Excel features may not be preserved.
